title: The Silk Road
puzzle: Was the Silk Road really one road, or something much bigger?
insight: The Silk Road was a vast network of trade routes linking Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and Europe through the movement of goods, ideas, and disease.
key_ideas: Silk and spices traveled with merchants.
Beliefs and technologies moved along the same routes.
Connection also spread outbreaks and conflict.
mind_blower: Long before the internet, trade routes were already tying distant societies into shared systems.
next_curiosity: If connection brings both opportunity and risk, how should we think about interdependence?
narration_text: Was the Silk Road really one road, or something much bigger? The Silk Road was a vast network of trade routes linking Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and Europe through the movement of goods, ideas, and disease. Silk and spices traveled with merchants. Beliefs and technologies moved along the same routes. Connection also spread outbreaks and conflict. Long before the internet, trade routes were already tying distant societies into shared systems. If connection brings both opportunity and risk, how should we think about interdependence?
